frontmatter.title: Savannah Wildlife Refuge
frontmatter.description: Photos from our visit to the Savannah National Wildlife Refuge — alligators, wetlands, and wildlife on the drive home from Florida.

photos.alt: Family group photo at the Savannah National Wildlife Refuge entrance sign
photos.caption: The whole crew at the Laurel Hill Wildlife Drive entrance — this was my fourth time, but their first.

photos.alt: Family posing at the Savannah National Wildlife Refuge sign
photos.caption: Bright sun, big smiles, and a refuge sign — the only acceptable group photo stop.

photos.alt: Closer group shot at the Savannah National Wildlife Refuge entrance sign
photos.caption: Everyone clustered around the sign, ready to go find some gators.

photos.alt: Family arranged around the refuge sign with kids sitting at the base
photos.caption: Kids on the sign, adults around it — the classic refuge photo.

photos.alt: Wide shot of the group at the refuge entrance sign with sun rays
photos.caption: Sun breaking through the clouds right as we arrived — felt like a good omen.

photos.alt: Wide group photo at the refuge sign with dramatic sky and sunbeams
photos.caption: That sky. That sign. That crew.

photos.alt: Bright clear group portrait at the Savannah National Wildlife Refuge sign
photos.caption: Full sun, full group — the sign says it all.

photos.alt: Backlit group photo at the refuge entrance sign with sunbeams
photos.caption: The sun was brutal but the photo still came out great.

photos.alt: Wide backlit group photo at the refuge sign under dramatic clouds
photos.caption: One more for the road — the clouds were incredible that day.

photos.alt: Family group photo at the Savannah National Wildlife Refuge entrance sign
photos.caption: Everyone together at the sign — this was the moment the trip really felt like an adventure.

photos.alt: Bright group portrait at the Savannah National Wildlife Refuge sign
photos.caption: The sun was blazing but everyone was smiling.

photos.alt: Backlit group photo at the refuge entrance sign with dramatic sunbeams
photos.caption: Sunbeams, Spanish moss, and the whole crew — hard to take a bad photo here.

photos.alt: Alligator basking on a log with its mouth open
photos.caption: An American alligator rests on a sunlit fallen log, mouth open as it basks beside calm water dotted with floating vegetation.

photos.alt: Alligator stretched out on a long log in the wetlands
photos.caption: A wider view of the wetland shows an American alligator stretched out on a long log, jaws open, surrounded by shallow water and reeds.

photos.alt: Broad marsh landscape with water channels and lily pads
photos.caption: A broad marsh landscape under bright sun, with shimmering water channels, lily pads, and distant tree lines across the refuge.

photos.alt: Quiet wetland pool with tall marsh grasses
photos.caption: Tall marsh grasses and reeds frame a quiet, reflective wetland pool covered with patches of floating vegetation.

photos.alt: Close-up of marsh water with floating debris and small dark shapes breaking the surface
photos.caption: If you look close enough, there's always something moving in there.

photos.alt: Close-up of an alligator basking on a log
photos.caption: Close-up of an American alligator basking on a weathered log, mouth wide open, its armored back and tail highlighted by the sun.

photos.alt: Alligator on a log with mouth open, calm reflective water beside it
photos.caption: Mouth open, eyes watching — they never miss a thing.

photos.alt: Large alligator stretched out on a sun-bleached log in the marsh
photos.caption: Sprawled out on the log like it owned it — because it did.

photos.alt: Alligator lying low on a narrow log in shallow water
photos.caption: Low and nearly invisible — you have to look twice to spot them.

photos.alt: Alligator sunning on a log surrounded by marsh grasses
photos.caption: A large alligator rests with its mouth open on a weathered log at the edge of the water, surrounded by marsh grasses and bright reflections.

photos.alt: Alligator resting on a bank at the edge of calm marsh water with reeds nearby
photos.caption: Chilling on the bank like it owns the place — which, honestly, it does.

photos.alt: Broad wetland scene with still water, reeds, and an alligator near the edge
photos.caption: The wetlands stretch out forever — and there's always a gator if you look closely enough.

photos.alt: Wide wetland scene with an alligator resting on a long log near the bottom
photos.caption: Spot the gator. It took the kids about two seconds.

photos.alt: Sunlit marsh panorama across the refuge
photos.caption: The low sun shines over the broad wetlands, sparkling across open water, lily pads, and wind-bent marsh grasses with a distant tree line on the horizon.

photos.alt: Cypress swamp with Spanish moss and still water
photos.caption: Bald cypress trees draped with Spanish moss stand in shallow, still water, their trunks and hanging moss reflected among duckweed and marsh plants.

photos.alt: Alligator warning sign beside dark swamp water
photos.caption: A posted alligator warning sign stands beside dark swamp water beneath tall cypress trees and hanging Spanish moss, with sunlight glinting off the water.

photos.alt: Alligator hidden among tall marsh grasses
photos.caption: An American alligator lies low and partly concealed among tall green grasses, mud, and floating vegetation, blending into the shaded edge of the wetland.

photos.alt: Sandy refuge road curving through live oaks with Spanish moss canopy
photos.caption: Driving through a tunnel of live oaks — Spanish moss hanging like curtains on every side.

photos.alt: View from inside a vehicle driving under massive live oaks draped in Spanish moss
photos.caption: The refuge road felt like driving through another world — moss everywhere, light filtering through.

photos.alt: Live oaks arching over the wildlife drive with Spanish moss
photos.caption: Large live oaks arch above the wildlife drive, their branches heavy with long curtains of Spanish moss, creating a tunnel-like entrance through the forest.

photos.alt: Alligator watching from the water with only eyes visible
photos.caption: An American alligator floats almost completely submerged, with only its eyes, snout, and part of its head visible above the rippling water beside aquatic plants.

photos.alt: Alligator swimming across open water
photos.caption: A young American alligator glides through blue, wind-ruffled water, its eyes and snout above the surface while its body remains mostly hidden below.

photos.alt: Alligator swimming through rippled blue water
photos.caption: An American alligator swims through rippled blue water, only its head, eyes, and part of its back visible above the surface as it moves quietly through the refuge.

photos.alt: Alligator swimming through calm brownish wetland water near the grassy bank
photos.caption: This gator glided right past us — just eyes and ridges breaking the surface.

photos.alt: Alligator eyes and snout barely breaking the surface of murky green water
photos.caption: Just the eyes and snout — they really do look like floating logs until you look closer.

photos.alt: Alligator head floating just above the water surface among small plants
photos.caption: You can see the ripple pattern fanning out from where it broke the surface.

photos.alt: Tight close-up of alligator head floating in water with eyes and nostrils above the surface
photos.caption: Those eyes — always watching, always still.

photos.alt: Alligator swimming through rippled water near green shoreline plants
photos.caption: Swimming right past the car — close enough to see every scale.

photos.alt: Close-up of alligator head poking through brownish-green wetland water
photos.caption: That slick brown shape in the water? Yeah, that's a gator.

photos.alt: Distant alligator just breaking the water surface
photos.caption: A small alligator head and snout peek out from choppy water, blending into dark blue ripples with a few marsh stems nearby.

videos.alt: Alligator swimming through the water at Savannah National Wildlife Refuge
Turtle in the water at Savannah National Wildlife Refuge
